hero_subtitle: SFTY supports government procurement with a flexible platform that preserves data sovereignty while connecting multi-provider outputs, secure workflows, and audit-ready oversight.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_description: Government teams face delays and risks when supervisory platforms impose vendor lock-in or lack sovereign deployment options. This limits flexibility in integrating existing providers, securing audit logs and controlling access across sensitive digital asset supervision environments.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY supports government teams with a secure, role-based platform that integrates multiple providers, enforces audit log governance, and delivers phased deployment options to safeguard sovereign data control.
workflow_title: From deployment setup to audit log control
workflow_description: SFTY supports controlled government deployment by managing access, permissions, audit logs and evidence governance within a phased, vendor-neutral infrastructure model.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another vendor lock-in. One sovereign control layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides public authorities a vendor-neutral supervisory layer that preserves data sovereignty, supports phased deployment, and integrates with existing government infrastructures securely.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY delivers a secure, vendor-neutral platform offering data sovereignty, role-based access, evidence-grade logging and procurement-safe architecture for government environments.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How does SFTY support secure sovereign infrastructure deployment
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how role-based access controls enable secure supervision
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit logs preserve evidence for regulatory reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe the benefits of a vendor-neutral model for procurement
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for government procurement?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a secure supervisory layer designed for public-sector procurement, supporting phased deployment, access governance, data sovereignty and audit logs in regulated digital asset environments.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support vendor-neutral supervision?
faq_2_answer: SFTY connects multiple provider outputs into connected supervisory workflows without replacing them, enabling governments to maintain flexibility and resilience through a vendor-neutral orchestration model.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit and evidence logs?
faq_3_answer: SFTY maintains evidence-grade logging, case history and decision records to support audit readiness and defensible supervisory reviews within controlled and secure environments.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: SFTY supports integration with government registries, reporting channels and internal systems through modular architecture designed for controlled deployment and compliance operations.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC, KYT or Travel Rule providers but structures their outputs into consolidated supervisory workflows and evidence packages.
final_description: Explore how SFTY supports phased deployment, role-based access and audit logs within secure government digital asset supervision.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
